key_risks: A sharp rebound in semiconductors/AI mega-caps could cause the S&P 500 benchmark to outrun this defensively tilted portfolio.
July 2 jobs report surprise could whipsaw regional banks and mid-caps on rate repricing.
Healthcare rally could mean-revert after sharp 7d/30d gains if EMA optimism fades.
Strait of Hormuz escalation could spike volatility and pressure cyclical holdings.

portfolio.rationale: Healthcare led the market on June 26 (+7.8% 7d) on EMA approval news; defensive low-beta with positive catalyst momentum and at 52w high, supported by the briefing's EMA committee recommendation fact.
KRE strong (+5.4% 7d, at 52w high) amid steepening curve and benign claims; positioning supported by improving macro and rate context, not price alone.
Defensive low-beta factor (+3.85% 7d) cushions against AI/tech-led index drawdown into the July 2 jobs report week.
Mid caps showed broad-based strength (+0.58% 7d, near 52w high) with healthier breadth than mega-cap indexes; benefits from solid macro data.
Cash-like buffer ahead of July 2 jobs report and holiday-shortened week to limit volatility exposure.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward defensive and broadening-breadth segments (healthcare catalyst, regional banks, low-vol, mid-caps) while avoiding the AI/semiconductor weakness dragging cap-weighted indexes, with a short-duration buffer into a jobs-report and holiday week.
rationale_summary: Favor catalyst-supported healthcare and breadth-positive non-mega-cap exposure over AI-heavy indexes, with defensive cushioning.
